response.justification: Bogdan has a genuinely relevant background in high-throughput distributed systems and current hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq, which maps well to the AI-native environment described. However, Python is entirely absent from his skills and employment history despite being a must-have requirement, which is a hard gap for this role. His distributed systems depth is real but dated (Dunnhumby, 2018–2020), and his more recent work is primarily TypeScript product engineering. The AI exposure is encouraging but only a few months old. Worth a screening call to probe Python proficiency and the depth of his technical strategy experience before ruling him out.
strengths: Proven high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems work handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby, including async I/O, caching, and microservices at scale
Active, hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work including MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks at current role — direct alignment with the AI-native engineering environment
Broad multi-language, multi-paradigm background with Staff-level titles and evidence of software craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD)
concerns: Python is completely absent from the skills list and every role description — this is a stated must-have and represents a significant, unmitigated gap
Distributed systems depth is dated (2018–2020); post-2020 work is TypeScript product and pricing-team feature engineering with no clear return to high-throughput infrastructure
No explicit evidence of cross-functional stakeholder management, technical strategy ownership, or bridging engineering and product at organizational scale

key_factors.factor: Current AI-agent and MCP tooling work
